task_completion.visual_evidence: The title 'Datacenter Thermal Analysis' is visible at the top of the Graphite dashboard interface.

task_completion.description: Graph titled 'Temperature with Thresholds' is created with the temperature metric and two threshold lines
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the exact title 'Temperature with Thresholds' is visible, showing a fluctuating metric line and two straight horizontal threshold lines at y=80 and y=100. The legend or metric editor should show the threshold functions.

task_completion.description: Graph titled 'Thermal-CPU Correlation' is created with temperature and scaled CPU metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the exact title 'Thermal-CPU Correlation' is visible, showing two distinct fluctuating lines. The legend should indicate 'servers.datacenter.machine_temperature' and 'EC2-1 CPU (scaled)'.

task_completion.description: Graph titled 'Temperature Rate of Change' is created showing the derivative of the temperature
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the exact title 'Temperature Rate of Change' is visible. The line should represent a rate of change (often fluctuating around zero or showing spikes), and the legend or metric editor should show the 'derivative' function applied to the temperature metric.

task_completion.description: The dashboard is saved with all three graphs present
task_completion.visual_evidence: All three completed graphs are visible together in the dashboard view, and there is an indication that the dashboard has been saved (e.g., a save confirmation notification or the absence of unsaved changes indicators).

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite GUI to create the dashboard and graphs
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Graphite web interface being interacted with (e.g., clicking menus, adding metrics, configuring graphs), rather than just terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual time-series data
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs show complex, realistic fluctuating lines indicative of real server metrics, not just empty graphs, straight lines (except for thresholds), or static mock data.

integrity.description: The agent didn't bypass the UI by writing a JSON dashboard file in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen building the dashboard within the browser-based Graphite UI, not editing a JSON file in a terminal or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Dashboard Name: 'Datacenter Thermal Analysis'. Expected Graph Titles: 'Temperature with Thresholds', 'Thermal-CPU Correlation', 'Temperature Rate of Change'. Graph 1 must show threshold lines at 80 and 100. Graph 2 must show a scaled CPU metric labeled 'EC2-1 CPU (scaled)'. Graph 3 must show a derivative function applied to the temperature metric. The data used is real-world time-series data from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark.
